message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Adding opt-in debug/trace logging to StreamListener (start/end detection, flush, reset). Only touching streaming_listener.py. Please confirm if your feature needs this file too so we can split cleanly.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: I'm implementing idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat feature, confined to streaming_listener.py (StreamListener class). If you need is_last_chunk on StreamResponse, please own messages.py; I'll defensively set is_last_chunk on the timeout StreamResponse only if the field exists on the dataclass, so no need to touch streaming_listener.py for that. Let me know if you need to touch streaming_listener.py too so we can split more precisely (e.g. by method).

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both need streaming_listener.py (mine: debug/trace logging, additive - new __init__ params debug/debug_logger, guarded log lines inside existing receive()/flush()/reset logic, no new methods, no signature changes to existing methods). I don't need messages.py at all (no StreamResponse schema changes). Since we can't cleanly split one file, let's serialize: I'll make my small additive edit first (just log lines + 2 optional __init__ params), send DONE, then you layer your idle_timeout_s/tick() work on top of my version and send your own DONE. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both genuinely need this file, so let's split by region instead of by file. My changes: (1) __init__ signature -> add idle_timeout_s: float | None = None param + self._last_chunk_ts = None attribute; (2) inside receive(), ONE new line right after 'chunk_message = chunk.choices[0].delta.content' / None-check to refresh self._last_chunk_ts on non-empty chunks; (3) a brand-new tick(self, now_ts=None) method appended after flush() at the end of the class. Please add your debug/trace logging anywhere else (start/end identifier detection, the allow_reuse reset branch, flush()) but avoid rewriting my tick() method or the __init__ signature line. I'll go first with a small, additive patch; ping me DONE-side when you're about to start so we don't stomp on each other's concurrent edits, or just add your logging as print/logger calls that don't touch the lines I listed. Sound OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed - serialize. You go first (debug/trace logging + debug/debug_logger __init__ params), send DONE when finished, then I'll layer idle_timeout_s/tick() on top and send my own DONE. I won't touch the file until I see your DONE.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed. Region split: you own __init__ idle_timeout_s param + _last_chunk_ts line + new tick() method; I own debug/debug_logger params, logging lines in receive() (start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, allow_reuse reset) and flush(). Go ahead and make your edit first and send DONE - I'll wait, then re-read the file fresh and layer my additive changes in afterward so we don't stomp on each other's concurrent edits.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: OK, I'll go first: __init__ idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts + one line in receive() to refresh it on non-empty chunk_message + new tick() method appended after flush(). Will send DONE shortly, then it's your turn.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished my part: added idle_timeout_s param + _last_chunk_ts init in __init__, one line in receive() right after the chunk_message None-check to refresh _last_chunk_ts when chunk_message is truthy, and a new tick(now_ts=None) method appended right after flush() (before find_predictor_for_stream_listeners). tick() defensively adds is_last_chunk=True to the StreamResponse only if that field exists on the dataclass, so your messages.py work (if any) isn't required for this to work, and there's no hard dependency either way. State reset in tick() is unconditional per the spec (ignores allow_reuse). File compiles cleanly. Your turn - go ahead and layer your logging in now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished debug/trace logging changes: added debug/debug_logger params to __init__, guarded DEBUG-level logs for start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, and allow_reuse state reset, plus a _truncate_for_preview helper and DEBUG_PREVIEW_LIMIT=80 constant. All existing tests pass (15 passed, 3 skipped) and ruff is clean. File is free for you to layer idle_timeout_s/tick() on top now.
